LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT LOIS SCHWARTZ'S DEMURRER TO SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT SCHWARTZ'S DEMURRER SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. NO FACTS ALLEGED THAT SCHWARTZ MADE MISREPRESENTATIONS WHICH INDUCED PLAINTIFF TO ENROLL OR CONTINUE HIS STUDIES AT NEW COLLEGE. PLAINTIFF ADMITS HE SUFFERED NO DAMAGES AS A RESULT OF NEW COLLEGE'S LOSS OF ACCREDITATION, AND NO FACTS PLED THAT DEFENDANT HAD ANY ROLE IN NEW COLLEGE'S DISSEMINATION OF INFORMATION TO PROSPECTIVE STUDENTS. PLAINTIFFS HAVE NOT ALLEGED A CONTRACT BETWEEN THEMSELVES AND DEFENDANT TO IMPLY THE EXISTENCE OF THE COVENANT OF GOOD FAITH AND FAIR DEALING. FACTS ALLEGED DO NOT SHOW ARBITRARY AND CAPRICIOUS GRADING AND TESTING. NO FACTS ALLEGED SHOWING DEFENDANT'S INVOLVEMENT WITH FRAUDULENT ADVERTISING AND CIVIL CONSPIRACY TO OBTAIN FEDERAL LOANS. THE STATEMENTS ALLEGEDLY MADE BY DEFENDANT ARE NOT STRONG ENOUGH TO SUPPORT A FINDING OF INTENTIONAL INFLICTION OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS. NO REASONABLE PROBABILITY THAT THE DEFECTS CAN BE CURED BY AMENDMENT. (DEFENSE COUNSEL WILL PREPARE ORDER.) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS PETER GABEL, ED ROYBAL, AND, CHRIS KANIOS DEMURRER TO SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEMURRER SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. AS TO FIRST, THIRD, FOURTH AND FIFTH CAUSES OF ACTION, PLAINTIFF OFFERS NO SPECIFICS AS TO HOW AMENDMENT COULD CURE THESE CLAIMS. AS TO SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION, SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT ALLEGES THAT FALSE REPRESENTATIONS OCCURRED "ON OR ABOUT THE BEGINNING OF THE 2004 SCHOOL YEAR" AND THAT ACCREDITATION WAS LOST ABOUT APRIL 2007. NO FALSE STATEMENT MADE TO SUPPORT A FRAUD CLAIM. NO SUFFICIENT DUTY ALLEGED ON THE PART OF THE DEMURRING DEFENDANTS TO DISCLOSE NEW COLLEGE'S ALLEGED TENUOUS STANDING WITH THE WASC. NO REASONABLE PROBABILITY THAT DEFECT CAN BE CURED BY AMENDMENT. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-28-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS PETER GABEL, ED ROYBAL, AND, CHRIS KANIOS DEMURRER TO SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT. Appearances: Amanda L. Ebey, Esq. Attorney at Law, (415) 989-8070 appearing for Defendants and moving party. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Demurrer sustained without leave to amend. As to first, third, fourth and fifth causes of action, plaintiff offers no specifics as to how amendment could cure these claims. As to second cause of action, second amended complaint alleges that false representations occurred "on or about the beginning of the 2004 school year" and that accreditation was lost about April 2007. No false statement made to support a fraud claim. No sufficient duty alleged on the part of the demurring defendants to disclose New College's alleged tenuous standing with the WASC. No reasonable probability that defect can be cured by amendment. Order signed in open Court. (302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine CSR 5797

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-10-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT LOIS SCHWARTZ'S DEMURRER TO SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT. Appearances: Arthur R. Abelson, Attorney at Law, (510) 351-8300, appearing for Defendant and moving party. There are no other appearances. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Defendant Schwartz's demurrer sustained without leave to amend. No facts alleged that Schwartz made misrepresentations which induced plaintiff to enroll or continue his studies at New College. Plaintiff admits he suffered no damages as a result of New College's loss of accreditation, and no facts pled that defendant had any role in New College's dissemination of information to prospective students. Plaintiffs have not alleged a contract between themselves and defendant to imply the existence of the covenant of good faith and fair dealing. Facts alleged do not show arbitrary and capricious grading and testing. No facts alleged showing defendant's involvement with fraudulent advertising and civil conspiracy to obtain federal loans. The statements allegedly made by defendant are not strong enough to support a finding of intentional infliction of emotional distress. No reasonable probability that the defects can be cured by amendment. (Defense counsel will prepare Order.)(302/CWW/PL)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR 5759
